What is the main objective of this docum.text: Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) combines retrieval and generation to produce grounded responses. Chunking strategy directly impacts retrieval quality because chunk boundaries control context granularity.

Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) combines retrieval and generation to produce grounded responses. Chunking strategy directly impacts retrieval quality because chunk boundaries control context granularity.

Fixed-size chunking is simple but can break semantic flow. Overlapping chunking preserves context across boundaries and may improve recall. Semantic chunking uses meaning-based boundaries and often improves coherence.
